items.title: Chief of Digital Services
items.org: Family & Medical Leave Insurance (FAMLI), Maryland Department of Labor
items.description: Third hire to the Digital Service team building out a greenfield digital-first service to support Maryland workers and employers in the rollout of paid family and medical leave for the state. Promoted in first year to lead the overall team of 17 state employees with expertise in product leadership, service design, content strategy, engineering, accessibility, and devops.
leadership: Led a cross-functional digital services team embedded in state government — navigating complex program requirements, facilitating inclusive, consultative processes that developed shared understanding across policy and technology stakeholders, and shaping strategy for modern service delivery

Designed and owned program governance for a complex multi-year initiative, including tracking interdependencies across teams, surfacing risks early, and delivering regular executive-ready status communications to senior leadership
Scaled team of product, engineering, and design professionals from 3 to 17, including developing hiring strategy and processes and onboarding systems
Coached and managed 9 senior members of the Digital Services team, including entire product team and other managers
delivery: Led build vs. buy strategy and oversaw $15.6M in annual vendor contracts, coordinating cross-functional alignment and partnering with state cybersecurity governance on foundational decisions around tech stack, tooling, and third-party tools/services

Coordinated cross-functional launch readiness for the program, including aligning communications,  outreach, customer support, and delivery teams ahead of key milestones and ensuring all stakeholders were consulted and informed
Established standards and documentation for the engineering team, specifically around accessibility
culture: Built team operating rhythms for a fully remote, cross-functional team, including meeting structure and async documentation practices that supported team health as we scaled

items.title: Engineering Manager, Data Integrations
items.description: Founded in 2012, Panorama Education helps students achieve academic success and well-being and gives schools and school districts a platform to hear what families, students, and teachers need in the classroom.
leadership: Led change management for significant organizational shifts (including new team structures and revised processes), communicating proactively and adjusting course based on team feedback
Refined the engineering career ladder and promotion process, creating a system that gave engineers clear, equitable paths to growth
Coached and developed mainly senior engineers, including facilitating promotions for 4 engineers over 3 quarters
delivery: Led strategy and delivery for 3 separate cross-functional teams in partnership with product and design, setting clear goals, maintaining delivery health, and ensuring impact for users in 1,500+ school systems nationwide
Developed new strategy and processes for how the engineering team prioritized and resolved customer support requests that improved cross-functional communication and overall efficiency
culture: Rebooted a dormant affinity group for women, gender-nonconforming and nonbinary employees, providing a point of connection and support for team members

items.title: Engineering Manager, Front-end
items.description: Founded in 2012, Lumen Learning uses open educational resources (OER) to provide affordable course materials that improve learning and replace expensive textbooks in high-enrollment college courses. Over 400 institutions across the U.S. use Lumen's award-winning digital courseware for in-person, online, and blended courses.
leadership: Designed the engineering career framework from scratch, including defining leveling criteria, role expectations, and growth pathways that gave the team clarity and the organization a foundation for equitable performance management
Scaled front-end team from 2 to 4 engineers, building hiring and onboarding processes rigorous and successful enough that the broader engineering org adopted them as standard practice
Collaborated closely with product managers, developers, and designer to scope and estimate work to achieve our product roadmap
Coached 5 team members from junior to senior levels
delivery: Provided guidelines to developers and content team for creating inclusive and accessible markup and about testing with screen readers
Writing documentation and participating in code review
culture: Conducted company-wide workshop on accessibility and inclusive design principles
Established and modeled engineering team norms, specifically around learning, professional development, and employee wellness

items.title: Adjunct Professor
responsibilities: Instructing undergraduate students in GD 220 (Graphic Design 2) and GD 431 (Front End Web development)
Developing curriculum and assignments for each 16-week course
Working 1:1 with students and providing detailed code review in response to assignments

items.title: Front-end Lead + Manager
items.description: Fractured Atlas builds software and provides services to help over 500,000 artists and arts organizations handle the business side of their work, like raising money, tracking donors, and selling event tickets.
leadership: Served as project lead for crowdfunding and fundraising tool (over $25 million annually)
Managed UX engineering team, including holding 1:1s and setting team priorities
delivery: Built and deployed design system to establish a consistent look and feel across a variety of applications
Created site wireframes and prototypes to improve internal discussions and planning about site features and organization
culture: Developed and presented all-staff training on accessibility testing and best practices
Provided guidance on the organization's transition to being wholly virtual/remote (including being featured on our blog)

items.title: Senior Front-end Developer
items.description: Detroit Trading Company aggregates in-market automotive shopper intelligence, providing data that is responsible for more than $1 billion in monthly car sales.

items.title: Freelance Web Designer + Developer
items.description: Designed and coded web sites for clients, including small and mid-size businesses, nonprofits, educational institutions, and individuals. Majority of projects were bespoke WordPress sites, including custom themes, custom content types and custom plugin development. Handled lead generation, client relations, and education/training.

items.title: Superpowered Front-end Theming with Sass & SMACSS Workshop
National Women In Tech Nonprofit
items.description: Baltimore chapter leader and teacher from 2013–2017. Organized and presented hands-on workshops for women to learn web and software development. Coordinated teachers and volunteer teaching assistants across a wide variety of tech subjects. Built relationships with local companies and nonprofits who can provide space for our classes or other sponsorship opportunities. At the time I stepped down, the chapter had 1500+ members and had hosted 60+ events.

items.title: Creative Mixtape
items.description: Collaborative events between 7 Baltimore professional design associations. Participated in event and sponsorship logistics, including development of the one-page event site for our kickoff December 2016 event.

items.title: Bmore on Rails, AIGA, Mesh Baltimore
ChangeEngine
items.description: Wrote a column on the intersection of art and measurable impact from 2012–2014 (archive of articles)

items.title: Tech Mission Org of the Year
PASSHE Women's Consortium Annual Undergraduate Women's Leadership Institution Scholarship
